candidates.text:
The journey begins with a chaotic bus ride where the driver insists on playing heavy metal at maximum volume, causing students to dance in the aisles while clutching their backpacks like they’re holding onto a life raft. Upon arrival, the first order of business is to locate the campus’s legendary “Secret Taco Stand,” a mythical food truck rumored to appear only when the moon is full and the Wi-Fi is down. Students must navigate through a maze of construction zones and confused pigeons to find it, with the first person to return with a taco winning a lifetime supply of free laundry detergent.

***

Day two kicks off with a “Survival of the Fittest” orientation, where students are split into teams and tasked with finding the most comfortable chair on campus—a mission that involves climbing lamp posts, hiding in library stacks, and negotiating with squirrels for prime seating. The afternoon is dedicated to the “UCI Obstacle Course,” a series of challenges including dodging sprinklers, solving riddles written in invisible ink, and outrunning a very persistent golden retriever named “Professor Fluff.” The team that completes the course fastest earns the title of “Campus Champions” and the privilege of choosing the menu for the next group meal.

***

The final day is all about “Chaos and Celebration.” Students must participate in the “Great Balloon Drop,” where hundreds of balloons are released into the sky, and the goal is to pop as many as possible before they disappear into the horizon. Afterward, everyone gathers for a “Reverse Picnic,” where they must eat their meals while balancing on one foot and reciting their favorite song lyrics backward. The day ends with a bonfire under the stars, where students share their wildest stories and make a pact to never, ever forget the absurdity of their three-day adventure.

***

As the sun sets on the third day, students board the bus with tear-streaked faces and stomachs full of questionable food, knowing they’ve survived the ultimate test of camaraderie and chaos. They exchange hugs, promises of future meetups, and a collective sigh of relief that the “UCI Experience” is finally over. As the bus pulls away, a single balloon floats up into the sky, symbolizing the end of an unforgettable journey—one that will be whispered about in hushed tones for years to come.

Day one kicks off with a high-speed chase through the campus quad, where students must dodge flocks of wild ducks while pretending to be in a spy movie. The mission? Locate the legendary "Hidden Coffee" machine rumored to brew espresso that makes gravity optional. After a brief nap in the library’s “Silent Disco Zone,” everyone attends a mandatory “How to Be a Robot” workshop, complete with beeping sounds and wobbly walks.

***

Day two is all about the legendary “UCI Taco Trivia” event, held in a parking lot that somehow doubles as a dance floor. Participants must answer questions like “What color is a cactus’s favorite superhero?” and “If a duck could speak, what would its first word be?” Winners get a golden burrito and a lifetime supply of “I Survived the Taco Trivia” stickers. Afterward, the group embarks on a “Silent Scavenger Hunt” where everyone must communicate only through interpretive dance.

***

Day three brings the grand finale: the “UCI Time Traveler’s Tea Party.” Students must brew tea using only a spoon, a leaf, and a single drop of “mystery liquid” (which is actually just water with a tiny splash of lemon). The tea is then served to a statue of a confused owl, who grants the group “honorary time traveler” status. The day ends with a “Reverse Picnic,” where everyone eats lunch in reverse order—starting with dessert and ending with an empty plate.

***

As the group packs up, they’re handed a “UCI Survival Certificate” that reads: “You have successfully survived 72 hours of absurdity, confusion, and questionable life choices.” The bus ride home is spent in silence, except for one student who keeps whispering, “The ducks are watching.”

The adventure begins at 5:00 AM with a bus ride so slow it feels like watching paint dry in reverse. Upon arrival, students will immediately attempt to locate the campus using only a napkin with "UCI" written in crayon, inevitably leading them to a nearby orange grove where they will spend the first two hours trying to convince squirrels they are actually oranges.

***

Day two is dedicated to "academic immersion," which involves wandering into the library and staring intently at a single book titled "The History of Glue" for six hours straight, convinced they’ve uncovered a Nobel Prize–worthy breakthrough. Lunch will be a scavenger hunt for the legendary “Orange Juice Fountain,” a myth so powerful that three students will actually try to drink from a decorative water feature, resulting in a very damp but very confused group.

***

The third day culminates in the “UCI Marathon of Nonsense,” where participants must run from one building to another while reciting random phrases like “I solemnly swear I am up to no good” and “This orange is my new best friend.” The race ends at the “Cafeteria of Infinite Sandwiches,” where every sandwich is either slightly burnt, slightly cold, or slightly sentient, but all are guaranteed to be eaten anyway.

***

The journey concludes with a group photo in front of a statue of a very tired-looking owl, which will then be framed and hung in the hallway of the school bus as a permanent reminder of their “epic academic pilgrimage.” As the bus departs, students will vow to never speak of this trip again, except when asked, at which point they will all simultaneously say, “It was… educational.”

Day one begins with an emergency meeting in the parking structure, where students are informed that the campus is actually a secret laboratory for studying the behavior of overenthusiastic squirrels. After a mandatory orientation involving the consumption of three energy drinks and a dance routine in front of a statue that definitely wasn’t there yesterday, the group is divided into teams and told to find the “hidden portal” to the student union, which turns out to be a very convincing cardboard cutout of a door.

***

Day two is dedicated to “interdisciplinary collaboration,” which means students from different majors must work together to solve the mystery of why the vending machines in the engineering building only dispense gummy bears and why the library’s Wi-Fi password is “Iguessso.” The day culminates in a “innovation fair,” where each team presents a prototype of a product that solves a problem no one has ever complained about, such as “a self-filling water bottle for people who don’t drink water.”

***

Day three is a “wellness retreat,” which involves a guided meditation in the cafeteria, a yoga session on the basketball court (which is also the designated spot for the campus’ famous “silent disco”), and a final “reflection circle” where students are asked to share one thing they learned about the campus that they will never forget. The day ends with a “graduation ceremony” where students are awarded a “UCI Survival Certificate” and a bag of gummy bears.

***

The trip concludes with a “farewell banquet” where students are served a meal that is 90% gummy bears and 10% mystery meat, and they are reminded that they are now “official UCI alumni” and that they should never, ever come back. As they leave, they are given a “UCI Alumni Card” which is actually just a business card for the campus’ new “Gummy Bear Emporium” and a “UCI Alumni Handbook” which is a 10-page pamphlet titled “How to Survive a Gummy Bear Apocalypse.”
